# Editorial & Research Evidence Dossier (Issue 012)

**Today's Date in Asia/Shanghai**: 2026-07-21  
**Curatorial Focus**: Feminist visual culture; photography, exhibitions, and photobooks; archival history and image mobilization; China and East Asia; AI-native creative workflows and pre-production technologies.

---

### Candidate 1: Visual Culture Signal
*   **Proposed Editorial Title**: 《Red Horse》夺魁：在废墟与日常的拼贴碎片中，重写战火之下的生命档案 
    *   *English Title*: "Red Horse" Wins the 2026 Kraszna-Krausz Photography Book Award: Redefining the Visual Archive of Survival and Daily Life Under Fire
*   **Specific Source URL**: [https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFwDssdJXrz64ftf-nHqil1X0n4kNneZDFGvytUQv23F6Vm5ET4ov990rQU1Uc4lmoWvuX3GX77W-FD9aV1Yi9d-pcDTJeM1NvehyC5dplAIEvHnng_ofKc3i-cMX_z7JpJGreyltXySVJ1ESsEmPkF](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQFwDssdJXrz64ftf-nHqil1X0n4kNneZDFGvytUQv23F6Vm5ET4ov990rQU1Uc4lmoWvuX3GX77W-FD9aV1Yi9d-pcDTJeM1NvehyC5dplAIEvHnng_ofKc3i-cMX_z7JpJGreyltXySVJ1ESsEmPkF) (Official Announcement page of Kraszna-Krausz Foundation Book Awards 2026)
*   **Publication Date**: 2026-06-25 (with events continuing into July 2026)
*   **Verified Facts**: Kyiv-based photographer Sasha Kurmaz has been named the winner of the 2026 Kraszna-Krausz Photography Book Award for his book *Red Horse* (published by Éditions Images Vevey). The book compiles an ongoing visual diary Kurmaz has kept since the full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022. It integrates personal documentary photographs, found imagery, hand-drawn sketches, notes, and physical street-salvaged ephemera, woven together in a raw collage format.
*   **Relevance**: Perfectly matches Muki’s interest in photobooks, material culture, and the role of photography as an ongoing record of personal and collective trauma. It demonstrates how "the collage" serves as an artistic tool to refuse sanitised or distant representations of war, turning a photobook into a tactile, living archive of survival.
*   **Duplication Assessment**: Completely new. While photobooks were featured in Issue 011 (Odette Elix England) and Issue 010 (riel Sturchio), this is the first coverage of the newly announced Kraszna-Krausz winner, shifting the geographical and political focus to contemporary Ukrainian image-making and wartime archiving.

---

### Candidate 2: AI Research / Creative Tech Signal
*   **Proposed Editorial Title**: 跨媒介叙事的“决策黏合剂”：Autodesk 研发 StoryArc 探索保留创作者意图的非线性创意工作流
    *   *English Title*: Preserving Narrative Intent Across the Creative Pipeline: Autodesk’s StoryArc Prototype Explores Non-Linear, AI-Assisted Workflows at SIGGRAPH 2026
*   **Specific Source URL**: [https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHL5-2UEBP05xWe9OKNMd5ItgFZ5buyFmHvfBemKInFFlibZji-IsekEh3fVMMCxmnka0E7PUk14rlKbJHSkjO7Rh3UiU4dEAAfPk1_XoqZOzjngZIw9Ixkr59nHd1UFfgMpg1GGiDqAN_sKRr-fFIpfVtn4auTHkJL3mh_jlL5Jrj4zo32R8pH1PzucOwCwLk](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQHL5-2UEBP05xWe9OKNMd5ItgFZ5buyFmHvfBemKInFFlibZji-IsekEh3fVMMCxmnka0E7PUk14rlKbJHSkjO7Rh3UiU4dEAAfPk1_XoqZOzjngZIw9Ixkr59nHd1UFfgMpg1GGiDqAN_sKRr-fFIpfVtn4auTHkJL3mh_jlL5Jrj4zo32R8pH1PzucOwCwLk) (Autodesk Research SIGGRAPH 2026 Announcement)
*   **Publication Date**: 2026-07-17 (SIGGRAPH runs July 19–23, 2026)
*   **Verified Facts**: Autodesk Research is demonstrating "StoryArc" at Booth #629 on the SIGGRAPH 2026 exhibition floor. StoryArc is an AI-powered research prototype designed to keep scripts, storyboards, 3D scenes, and previs (pre-visualizations) dynamically connected throughout the evolving pre-production pipeline. This allows non-linear creative decisions to propagate across assets without losing the artist’s original narrative intent.
*   **Relevance**: Aligns with the core interest in "AI-native workflows" and creative agency. Instead of utilizing AI to generate monolithic output (which often strips creators of agency), StoryArc treats AI as a relational database/interface layer that connects human decisions across different visual media (text, sketch, 3D).
*   **Duplication Assessment**: Completely new. Previous issues explored model-routing frameworks and prompt compilation; this targets a real-world pre-production workflow tool shown live during the current week of SIGGRAPH 2026.

---

### Candidate 3: Feminist Structure Signal
*   **Proposed Editorial Title**: 《June Givanni》荣膺移动影像图书奖：重构四十载黑人与泛非主义电影的“女性看护与反抗档案”
    *   *English Title*: "June Givanni: The Making of a Pan-African Cinema Archive" Wins the 2026 Kraszna-Krausz Moving Image Book Award: Reclaiming Forty Years of Women’s Curatorial Labor in Black Cinema
*   **Specific Source URL**: [https://events.maydayrooms.org/e/59/approaches-to-a-pan-african-cinema-archive-with-june-givanni-and-onyeka-igwe](https://events.maydayrooms.org/e/59/approaches-to-a-pan-african-cinema-archive-with-june-givanni-and-onyeka-igwe) (MayDay Rooms Archive Event Page)
*   **Publication Date**: 2026-06-25 (Award announcement)
*   **Verified Facts**: Onyeka Igwe has won the 2026 Kraszna-Krausz Moving Image Book Award for *June Givanni: The Making of a Pan-African Cinema Archive* (published by Lawrence Wishart). The book uses oral histories and archival deep dives to document the pioneering curatorial labor of Guyanese-born curator June Givanni. Her volunteer-run private archive (housed at London's MayDay Rooms) preserves over 10,000 films, posters, and scripts from Africa, the Caribbean, and the Black British diaspora, dating back to the early 1980s.
*   **Relevance**: Explores structural feminism, curatorial labor, and decolonial archive creation. Igwe conceptualizes Givanni's private archive as a "feminist counter-archive". It directly addresses how marginalized cultural histories are kept alive through invisible, precarious care labor by women curators rather than formal institutions.
*   **Duplication Assessment**: Completely new. Connects with the Jo Spence curation archive featured in Issue 011 but shifts focus to the preservation of moving-image traditions, Black British cinema, and post-colonial feminist archiving practices.

---

### Candidate 4: China / East Asia Signal
*   **Proposed Editorial Title**: 跨越一个世纪的底层与景观：香港公共图书馆巡展“梦周基金会早期中国摄影藏品”
    *   *English Title*: Mapping the Face of China a Century Ago: Hong Kong Public Libraries Stage Roving Exhibition of the Moonchu Foundation’s Early Chinese Photography Collection
*   **Specific Source URL**: [https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEQX_J157z-6Z45-N_lYx-NK1751XR4yynjP-GC4lIDKIdSHb4G1CYDF4XHzvM1zug4mUORXJo87WG86Ywvgg_VZp4sml1yfQ2njRvwo2PBnuJ4Ga4FuWB9qApX7X2RuP_3B-qcwBeinFP8tAWHreUrnU2bmcMpx9lnGfzeWRINpW6ewBIpcNUsRCgVjleBHVITe7zEe20Jc-3bJ6d9-9WKX5wP7JxwYQ](https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQEQX_J157z-6Z45-N_lYx-NK1751XR4yynjP-GC4lIDKIdSHb4G1CYDF4XHzvM1zug4mUORXJo87WG86Ywvgg_VZp4sml1yfQ2njRvwo2PBnuJ4Ga4FuWB9qApX7X2RuP_3B-qcwBeinFP8tAWHreUrnU2bmcMpx9lnGfzeWRINpW6ewBIpcNUsRCgVjleBHVITe7zEe20Jc-3bJ6d9-9WKX5wP7JxwYQ) (Official HKSAR Libraries Roving Exhibition Schedule)
*   **Publication Date**: 2026-07-13 (Roving schedules updated for July 2026)
*   **Verified Facts**: The Hong Kong Public Libraries (including Hong Kong Central Library and Smithfield Public Library) are currently hosting a roving exhibition throughout 2026 featuring selected works from the Moonchu Foundation’s Chinese photography collection. Donated to the Hong Kong Museum of History, this collection contains over 24,000 historical photographs, books, and periodicals spanning from the late 19th to the early 20th century. The exhibit displays rare glass negatives, stereoscopic photos, and hand-colored prints that document both major historical events (e.g., Opium Wars, Sino-Japanese War) and micro-narratives of urban landscapes and folk customs.
*   **Relevance**: Speaks directly to Muki’s interest in image history, archival preservation in China/East Asia, and the migration of historical photos from private foundations to public memory spaces. It demonstrates how early photographic materiality (glass, stereoscopes) mediates our understanding of historical reality.
*   **Duplication Assessment**: Completely new. While "Beijing Silvermine" in Issue 010 investigated vernacular everyday snapshots from late-20th-century China, this focuses on institutionalized late-Qing and early Republican-era media archaeology, addressing early chemical photography's material footprint.

---

### Candidate 5: Archive / Long Read / Opportunity
*   **Proposed Editorial Title**: 聆听浮游生物的微观劳动：李佳宝等联合创作《深坠》亮相 SIGGRAPH 2026，以 AI 重塑生态媒介档案
    *   *English Title*: Listening to the Microscopic Labor of Plankton: Jiabao Li’s "The Long Fall" Debuts at SIGGRAPH 2026, Melding Scientific Data, AI, and Ecological Memory
*   **Specific Source URL**: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fe-Xd3gxcsU](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fe-Xd3gxcsU) (Artist's Direct Video Documentation & Performance Description)
*   **Publication Date**: 2026-07-18 (Featured in SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery)
*   **Verified Facts**: Co-created by artist Jiabao Li (Northeastern Univ./Univ. of Tokyo), Manu Prakash (Stanford), Will Tallent (UT Austin), and Michael Bruner (Texas A&M), *The Long Fall: A Descent Into the Ocean's Living Memory* is an immersive audiovisual installation featured at the SIGGRAPH 2026 Art Gallery. It translates biological 3D datasets gathered by Stanford Prakash Lab's "Gravity Machine" and PlanktonScope over 18 ocean expeditions. The work features a physical "plankton instrument" with 100 responsive pads, bioluminescent dinoflagellates as living triggers, and a soundscape woven with the AI-reconstructed voice of pioneering marine biologist Rachel Carson reading her 1951 prose.
*   **Relevance**: Unites several of Muki's key nodes: ecological archiving (microscopic labor of ocean plankton), creative technology/AI-synthesis (restoring historical female scientific voices), and sensory media power. It reframes climate data as a poetic, tactile, and audible performance.
*   **Duplication Assessment**: Completely new. While ecological archives were briefly touched upon via Southeast Asian cinema (Issue 09), this introduces a groundbreaking synthesis of actual laboratory instruments, physical computation, and AI voice reconstruction at the intersection of media art and marine biology.
